title: What does she love most about her life
subtitle: A journal prompt set for celebrating the wealthy version of you from the inside out.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 28 of the MONEY program. Use to close a week of identity work by writing from the perspective of the wealthy version of you — what she loves, what she values, what her days feel like.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 28 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use to close identity work — to step fully into the wealthy version of you and write from that vantage point rather than looking at her from a distance.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 28 of the MONEY program. The prompts close the Week 4 identity arc: they ask you to step fully inside the wealthy version of you and write from there, not about her from the outside. Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What small luxuries, comforts, or joyful moments already make me feel abundant?
Find the things already present. Write at least three.
How would I celebrate and honour myself if I already were the wealthy version of me?
Write specific actions and choices, not attitudes.
What does she love most about her life?
Write it in first person as her: "I love..." Go for specifics — the Tuesday mornings, the people, the work.
What daily choices express who she is now?
Write the small choices: how she starts the morning, what she says no to, how she spends an ordinary hour.
What part of this identity will I carry into next week?
One specific thing. A decision, a habit, a way of speaking.
Adapted from Day 28 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
